behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Unit test runs the lint rule against template strings containing known phase-instruction snippets vs clean controls; Integration test wires full template loader + lint runner. Unit already catches the real failure in this behavior (rule logic failing to detect copied phase instructions). Lower level miss: none below Unit exists. Higher levels rejected: behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow because no OS/network/subprocess or model judgment is required.
behaviors.test_description: Add table-driven unit tests for the lint function/rule that flag copied phase-instruction patterns from SKILL.md and allow non-leaking templates.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Unit test checks only final-string scanner; Integration test executes real prompt assembly (template + interpolation + fixtures) then scans output. Real failure here is module handoff during assembly introducing leakage, which Unit on scanner alone misses. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond in-process wiring; no AI output dependence; no multi-agent chain. Higher levels rejected by behavior text "fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness" (assembly path), not live model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run harness prompt-construction pipeline with representative fixtures and assert assembled prompt has no copied phase instructions.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Unit test for a single loader function with mocked return; Integration test invokes test runner using real plugin/skill files and verifies assertions are driven by loaded context, not embedded rules. Lower-level miss: mocked loader can pass even if invocation wiring still uses hard-coded prompt instructions. Integration brake: no required real HTTP/subprocess; correctness target is context-source wiring, not model-output quality; no multi-step agent pipeline required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the invocation path with real skill files and assert provenance: expected behavior checks trace to loaded skill context, with no hard-coded phase rules in prompt text.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Unit test checks variant-selector function output; Integration test runs harness end-to-end (without live model) across baseline vs degraded/missing SKILL.md and confirms identical user prompt text with swapped context source. Lower-level miss: function-level test won’t catch prompt mutation or invocation-path coupling. Integration brake: this is local module/file wiring, not real OS event semantics, not model judgment, not multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize harness over skill variants (normal, degraded, missing), assert user-style prompt text is identical across runs and only skill context input changes.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Integration test with stubbed model can force pass/fail regardless of skill quality; Agentic test runs real model with baseline vs degraded skill content under identical prompt and expects regression signal. Lower-level miss: stubs hide whether model behavior truly depends on skill context. Rejection gate for Workflow: behavior describes a single behavioral outcome under one user-style prompt, not multiple real agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral smoke test twice (baseline skill vs degraded/removed section) using identical prompt text; assert degraded variant fails the behavior criteria.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Unit test for parser-order helper; Integration test executes harness response pipeline with a fake model response and verifies artifact write occurs before parse/extract stage. Lower-level miss: unit helper tests do not prove stage ordering across modules. Integration brake: no real network/subprocess needed; no dependence on model quality/content correctness; no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Instrument pipeline ordering in an integration test to assert raw response artifact creation precedes any JSON parse/extraction call.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Unit tests for assertion utilities using crafted outputs (keyword-present but structurally wrong, and structurally correct controls); Integration test runs full harness with canned outputs. The core failure is assertion logic weakness, which Unit catches directly; integration adds little for this behavior text. Higher levels rejected: no real infra or model dependence is required to validate structural-check logic.
behaviors.test_description: Add unit tests for assertion matchers that fail on misplaced fields/schema-path violations even when expected keywords are present.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Candidate tests: Integration test with deterministic fixtures can simulate mismatch cases; Agentic test uses live model runs where outputs may include keywords yet violate structure, proving structural assertions catch real regressions missed by keyword checks. Lower-level miss: fixture-based outputs cannot validate true model-driven regression patterns. Rejection gate for Workflow: behavior specifies live execution of one response-evaluation step, not a chained multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live-model tests with structural and keyword-only evaluators on the same prompts; assert cases exist where keyword-only passes but structural assertions fail.
